channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Old guy in Corvette cope — 84 replies — 336.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Old guy in Corvette cope

Open thread • Replies: 84 • Updated: 2026-05-04 04:48 UTC

OP

This will be YOU, just some cringe old fart finally getting his dream car after muh 401k investing for 40 years.

If you're not investing everything into Bitcoin RIGHT NOW to potentially make it in 10 years while you still have some youth left it's fucking over for you. I hope I make it live like a fucking king in my youth while you will be waging till 60 years old.

MAKE IT OR DIE

Early replies (first 3)

62181483
i would rather buy a corvette over buying bitcoin at 78K

62181491
>>62181462

No I bought my dream car 3 years ago and it got repossessed and emptied my 401k and have to declare bankruptcy. I'm only 35 though.

62181498
are we just glossing over the fact there's a fucking dinosaur in the background?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62183194
>>62181619
>work from home
im doing a survey of sorts. what are you doing that allows you to WFH after covid?

62183247
>>62181462
I’ve been driving a corvette since my late 20’s and I’m in my mid 30’s now. Skill issue.

62183296
>>62183194
IT

62183330
>>62181619
>460K
no one cares about your poorfag rubles

62183336
>>62183330
what a jewish thing to say.
if you are any race but jewish you should be ashamed of yourself. Wake up anon.

62183345
>>62181498
Don't worry, it's behind a fence.

62183408
>>62181462
This is literally that boomer wojak where he's like
>now that I'm in my 50s and completed all my life goals, I can finally get a gf

62183637
>>62181462
I retired at 24 and are back to waging at 36, not for long though.

62183644
If you dont drive a v8, lower your tone when talking to me

62183659
>>62183330
460K euros is almost 1M dollars, mutto

62184540
>>62181462
I have 15k in MSTR, bitcoin maxi chud, tell me, what will that be worth in 10 years ?

62184625
>>62181905
no

62184639
>>62181498
holy shit is that real

62184698
>>62183644
>V8
Pussy shit. Make it money is having a butler to drive you around with the V10. Touching a steering wheel is for the poor.

62184707
>rental
Few years pass and you would have already done a huge chunk of the payments.
RENTKEKS ARE SUBHUMANS

62185860
Got mine about a year ago. C7z06 with a clutch pedal. Every performance metric is way out of its league. Amazing gm can make something like a z06 and a Chevy equinox as well

My only complaint is the long ass gearing. It’s hard to have fun around town. You really gotta find a proper spot to do a nice 1-2-3 hit. 3rd gear tops out at like 123mph.

Also, fuck the gay ass nuvette. And also…. I’m in my fucking 40s now still waiting for the link yacht ho-down.

62185906
>>62181905
>>62181907
Inshallah

62185924
>>62185860
That looks sick man.
My ‘maro

62185929
>>62184698
Damn. Now this is what I call a proper bangwagon
>>62181462
Also, you must be an ABSOLUTE faggot if you still have shit like “muh dream car”
Car have two utilities
1- drive you
2- show women you have money

62185933
>>62183644
douchemobile

62185947
>>62181462
Is this like the pinnacle of boomer social status? A fucking meme sports car which deprecates as soon as it drives off the lot?

62185997
>>62185924
Awesome color and looking clean as hell.

62186032
>>62185924
Hell yeah brother. My wife has a 6th gen lt1. Nothing comes close to them in the price range. The alpha chassis is so good. I had a 4v blackwing prior to my z06 so I had some good seat time with an alpha chassis and a tr6060 manual.

Gonna be completely honest, I actually like the alpha chassis more than rhe c7 vette. Much easier to push hard at near the limit, and steering feels better/communicates more. Vette is obviously a bit faster around a track.

I really need to grab some 1le rep wheels for my wife’s lt1. They look sick. Good color choice too senpai.

Pic rel was my 4v blacking. I loved the car, it just should have came with a v8. I came from an f80 m3 comp prior. Both manual. 4vbw was much more dialed in… but that v6 tt was so blaaah

62186033
I didn't buy Bitcoin in 2013. Its over for me a loooong time ago.

62186039
>>62181782
>>62182527
good luck breaking 6.49 second quarter mile with a stick.

62186074
>>62186039
Good luck telling time with your Rolex

Good luck being a dj with antiquated technics 1200 turntables and vinyl records

Many people like mechanical/basic feels more. Nothing like rowing your own gears after pulling a 1.7 60’ and getting a clean pass. Some niggers just don’t get it and that’s ok

>>62186033
>he fell for the fud and hasn’t been stacking link. U have an opportunity rn that is only marginally worse than old link fags. Load the fuck up and be ready to hold another 40 years. Or 1 year.

62186078
>>62181462
My friend raced a guy in his corvette once and when we pulled upto the set of lights he wound down his window with the biggest smile and was hooting and hollering "whoooo boys good shit that was funnn!" he yelled like he was 16 again.

OP is a fag.

62186088
>>62186074
im just saying its like the bell curve meme, automatic is for women, stick is for men, automatic is for real men who go faster than 6.50

62186098
>>62186078
Can confirm
>I go to cars and coffee
>roll around in drag radials trying to get into shenanigans
I used to mock corvette owners…. But I think now I get them. To the burger American boomer, the corvette was the greatest car ever (they have Alzheimer’s concerning dodge vipers), and they feel like they “made it” when they have one to park in the garage and take out to Texas Roadhouse for a steak with the ol ball n chain.

If heard boomers say this phrase often
>vet is the greatest American sports car ever made, bar none. It’s boomers made it car
>you need yourself a got dang John deer, thats when you made it bud

62186111
>>62181462
uncmaxxing

item.title: It's Official -- Spirit Airlines Ceases All Operations Effective Immediately At 03:00 AM EST — 38 replies — 65.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
It's Official -- Spirit Airlines Ceases All Operations Effective Immediately At 03:00 AM EST

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-05-04 04:15 UTC

OP

R.I.P. to them yellow banana bois. Press F to pay respects.

https://www.spiritrestructuring.com/

Early replies (first 3)

62180860
>>62180856
rip. should be a sticky.

62180864
>>62180856
i thought orange nigger was going to bail them out with taxpayer dollars?

62180866
>>62180864
i guess you were wrong again

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62180926
>>62180907
You're welcome! I was never customer-facing but my job was just as important in getting planes from point A to point B. The pilots at Spirit were especially awesome. Very chill. The gate agents may have been terse and rude but it was usually because they were horrifically understaffed, which was a management issue. You try getting hundreds of people off and on an airplane in under an hour by yourself whilst getting yelled at by entitled poors. I would be rude too lol.

The business model worked well for a time but I came in late as the wheels really started to come off. I went there with plenty of experience to go to a Legacy carrier, but I was looking forward to the JetBlue merger since I grew up in the South Florida area and wanted the benefit of Major airline pay while being near family and friends, which the merger would have facilitated. Sadly, the airline industry is extremely fickle and cyclical, so them's the brakes. I sincerely hope all my former colleagues land on their feet. The everyday working people were great, management just sucked ass.

62180929
>>62180856
F, only few them once and they fucking sucked. But at least they kept the brown hoarde at bay.

62180938
>>62180908
The trick to Spirit is (was) being able to carry a backpack on with a bunch of packing cubes then you get a round trip for only $100. It was very fun when I would fly them (even before I worked for them) because FLL always had Broward Sheriff Officers close by to the Spirit gates in order to deter chimp outs. It usually didn't work but it was fun watching blacks get suplexed or thrown off flights over very trivial things every now and then.

62180943
Never had an exceptional flight
Never had a bad flight
It was Mediocre from day 1 and almost always the cheapest option. RIP

62180945
>>62180929
Personally, I think blacks and browns will just stop flying. The Big Four have an even bigger stranglehold on the market now and already said they will raise prices due to jet fuel costs; now that there's even less route competition in the Florida market, those prices are gonna skyrocket. It's going to be too expensive for the poors to fly, which is good since in my experience ULCC flyers are 80% scum of society and 20% polite savvy consumers. I feel bad for the latter but not the former.

62180951
>>62180908
>>62180938
>The trick to Spirit is (was) being able to carry a backpack on with a bunch of packing cubes then you get a round trip for only $100

Yeah this was the move with Spirit. However, if you signed up for their credit card, you could get up to 2 free checked bags per flight. The card itself is about 70 bucks per year but the first year was free. Now that I think about it, I wonder what happens to my spirit credit card

>>62180945
I think you're right, and I could see this being a boon for bus and rail travel especially. Amtrak will probably get a lot busier for routes that were once serviced by Spirit. Of course, it also takes longer, and in the age of minimal days off with PTO, it's not often an option for people. That being said, rail is the most resilient form of transport to oil price change since it is so efficient

62180955
>>62180945
I have always used Sun country for Florida flights and always found them cheaper than spirit, and I fly there twice a year, I fly with only a backpack

62180967
>>62180951
>Now that I think about it, I wonder what happens to my spirit credit card
If you check the link in OP there's another link that describes what happens to points and such. You might be fucked though.

>I think you're right, and I could see this being a boon for bus and rail travel especially.
Good point. I'm pretty sure the Brightline saw an uptick in ticket sales when Silver Airlines went under, since they mostly serviced the South Florida region. Amtrak will probably do well.

62180971
>>62180955
Sun Country, Allegiant, Breeze, Avelo and Frontier will likely get most of Spirit's previous clientele. The Legacy carriers are just going to keep getting more expensive.

62180976
>>62180967
>If you check the link in OP there's another link that describes what happens to points and such. You might be fucked though.

I only had 7,000 points left after my most recent round flight with Spirit two weeks ago. Unless I booked something over 6 months in advance, and even then, 7,000 points was maybe good for half the cost of a single flight. I don't care. I got 6 total flights from Spirit for a grand total of 200 bucks. If I don't get any back from those points that's fine, Spirit served me well, I feel like I got a great value already

62180978
>>62180945
I hope this is true but I doubt it. They'll just go into debt to fly like they go into debt to buy SUVs and shit.
Fuck, this sucks. I live in the south so the primary airlines were Spirit and Delta. Not only will Delta surely begin raising their prices but now I'll have to deal with even more niggers

62181006
>>62180945
Lol, lmao. Nobody curtails spending when prices of things go up, especially not groids.

62181048
>>62180856
They can sell all the jet engines for AI data centers.

62181053
Pour one out for the homie.

62181069
>>62180971
Maybe that's for the best. Spirit was a great way of keeping anti social behavior off the primary airlines.

62181075
>>62181069
She's sucking her gut in so hard for the picture that it could depressurize the whole joint.

62181250
recession indicator

62181569
>>62180875
>Legacy Airline Rejects
You are. Why would anyone choose to work for Spirit over Delta?
>Merger.
They tried with JetBlue. Would have made them 8% of the airline market. They got blocked by Congress for some fucking reason. It would have been less like Coke and Pepsi merging, and more like RC Cola merging with Ale-8.

62181590
I’m a former Air Force guy, and I loved Spirit. Flying 17 hour flights on a C-130 conditioned my body and mind to sleep anywhere in any position. I’d get my cheap Spirit ticket, stuff my necessities into my carry-on and pockets, and I’d sleep through the whole flight, no matter how loud Shaniqua was.

62181802
>>62180856
A long time coming unfortunately. They've declared bankruptcy several times over the last few years. The truth is that its just not a profitable business model to appeal to the lowest common denominator. What a lot of people don't know is the big carriers make a significant portion of revenue via credit card offers. And if you travel a lot particularly for work, the offers are quite good actually. But Spirit's 'clientele' are typically very poor and have bad credit.

62182163
>>62181802
>>62181802
So what you're saying is that if Spirit had pivoted into selling Hennie bottles and Bluetooth boomboxes financed through Klarna in flight they'd probably be okay?

62182165
>>62180875
How much do the shareholders get from dissolving the company?

62182491
>>62182163

62182508
>>62180908
>Now they'll move on to other airlines, just have to see over time

Idk my impression was a number of spirit flights were so cheap even after Jewish trickery they were attracting the Greyhound crowd. With them gone I think that group will largely avoid flying, or be in such small numbers they won't get out of hand.

62183202
>>62181569
>You are. Why would anyone choose to work for Spirit over Delta?
I already had enough experience to go to a Legacy carrier, but for me personally I was hoping to have my cake (get Major airline pay) and eat it too (live and work near family and friends) if the JetBlue merger went through, which obviously it didn't after a federal judge blocked it. It's a bunch of horseshit in hindsight because Alaska Airlines was allowed to acquire Hawaiian with zero issues. As for why many of my colleagues did it, it was good experience towards getting to a Major and most of them were South Florida natives with homes already so they weren't planning on leaving. After the past round of furloughs a a few months ago, many people finally jumped ship for the Majors even those who had been there for 15+ years at that point. That's how bad it got. I got out of there right after they declared bankruptcy the first time. As far as the "Legacy airline rejects" line goes, that ironically applied more to upper management who were too incompetent to go to a Major carrier and/or got fired from it and/or from smaller airlines they ran into the ground, then nepotistically brought other managers to wherever they go next. The airlines are a really small industry for a reason. Everybody knows everyone else.

62183218
>>62182165
Not as much as they would have from keeping it almost but definitely less if they took the government bailout. The problem is like this anon said >>62181802 it's been a long time coming. Once the post-COVID money ran out and consumers started to prefer a more premium flying experience (which is the opposite of Spirit's business model) after being cooped up for a year-plus made their business model not nearly as profitable. They also over leveraged themselves using that COVID money to buy more planes they couldn't afford. Then the Pratt & Whitney engine issues required significant grounding and maintenance inspections on the Airbus A321-neos that they just bought and now couldn't use. Then the federal government blocked the merger. Then finally the Iran War causing fuel costs to skyrocket was the final nail in the coffin.

>>62180976
They are (were) a great value if you traveled light and hunted for deals.

62183222
>>62180978
>>62181006
You guys are probably right. They will just switch to Frontier.

62183228
>>62181048
The Legacy carriers will likely gobble up Spirit's aircraft once they liquidate since there is an aircraft shortage as Boeing is incompetent and is still fixing shit they outsourced to jeets and Airbus just can't keep up with demand. I know United especially was eyeing Spirit's fleet like a ravenous vulture.

62184661
>>62183202
What’s it like experience wise now? Do you think someone starting a multi-crew turbine multi engine gig could get to a get within a few years over there? Not trying to be positive but sometimes you’ve got to close a door to open a window i.e. covid. Is late 20s too late to get into Jets there too?

62186044
>>62181569
ALE-8 MENTIONED
HANG ALL CHEERWINE ENTHUSIASTS
AVAILABLE ON ALL CONTINENTS

item.title: >IS- IS THAT... A CHAIR!?!? >NO! NUH UH. YOU CAN'T JUST SIT DOWN WHILE WORKING! WHEN I SAID TO "WORK SMARTER NOT HARDER"… — 53 replies — 45.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>IS- IS THAT... A CHAIR!?!? >NO! NUH UH. YOU CAN'T JUST SIT DOWN WHILE WORKING! WHEN I SAID TO "WORK SMARTER NOT HARDER"…

Open thread • Replies: 53 • Updated: 2026-05-04 03:39 UTC

OP

>IS- IS THAT... A CHAIR!?!?
>NO! NUH UH. YOU CAN'T JUST SIT DOWN WHILE WORKING! WHEN I SAID TO "WORK SMARTER NOT HARDER", I DIDNT MEAN LIKE THIS!!!!
>WHAT DO YOU MEAN YOURE TIRED!? THATS WHAT YOUR DESIGNATED 30 MINUTE BREAK IS FOR!!! WORK IS SUPPOSED TO BE AN 8 HOUR EXERCISE OF MISERY! THATS JUST HOW IT IS!!!
>D-DID YOU JUST SAY THAT IT DOESNT EFFECT YOUR EFFICIENCY!?!? DID YOU JUST POINT OUT THAT ITS 2AM AND THERE ARE NO GUESTS TO GIVE A SHIT?? THIS CANT BE HAPPENING!!!!!! THE POINT ISNT TO LOOK GOOD FOR CUSTOMERS, ITS TO UH- UM- PROVE YOUR A HARD WORKER!!!! BEING A HARD WORKER FOR ME IS THE MOST IMPORTANT THING EVER AND I SAID YOU CANT SIT WHILE YOU WORK!!!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62181647
>>62181643
I look like this and say this

62181651
>>62181643
i'm in my late 30s (white collar job etc.) but i still remember how fucking retarded retail/service industry managers were. even the district manager "elites" were insufferably fucking dumb retards.

62181656
>>62181643
Yes. Because you don't want employees to develop bad habits

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62181953
>>62181725
>>62181737
>You're pathetic if you don't bow to retail middle management

Cuckold

62182016
>hey from today on all our restaurant employees will have to pay for the food here they eat during their break

62182040
>>62182016
This is a personal huge pet peeve.

62182122
>>62181643
Deal in the real world, bitch.

62182166
>>62181953
>retail middle management
This has nothing to do with that.
If standing at your wagie job is an issue or you have a problem with it, you are definitely lower class.

62182176
>>62181824
What kind of attitude is this? lol.
I can’t even tell if you’re trolling. This is peak reddit tier posting.

62182189
>>62181895
I am lanky, I stay away from barbells. I stick with volleyball, swimming, table tennis, stuff like that.
My last office gig had standing desks. I would make resolutions to stand x hours every day just to rebound to sitting all the time again. In retail there is no chance of sitting which was awesome for me.

62182199
>>62181643
give me a job right now or I will kill you

62182202
Isn't standing healthier than sitting o algo?

62182298
>>62182202
Not if you’re an American.

62182391
>>62181643
Dude I think about this all the time at Amazon and it's one of the small indicators that this society is a retard dog and pony show. Sometimes I am waiting for 10 mins for my machine to be one and I think to myself "what if I could just SIT DOWN right now?" Heck my job and a lot of the other jobs could be done with a chair.

62182402
>>62181657
When I worked at a cannery in Alaska there was this 16 yo but he was very tall, maybe 6"6? Anyways he had a position of putting fish onto a conveyor belt and he was sitting on two stacked buckets and his long arms were doing all the work. Had a good pace and was doing good work. The manager waited till he got up and snatched those buckets with such rage I was surprised. Rarely saw him like that and it was usually for a good reason. The kid wondered what the issue was but we all knew that that's simply how it is when dealing in the real world. My manager doesn't even bust his ass physically at that job so idk what he was pissed about.

62182409
>>62181656
Are we animals? Are we children??? We can control what habits we develop just fine.

62182418
>>62182409
are we human or are we dancer

62182427
>>62182402
Idk, manager was mad he was sitting. What did the kid do after?
You can sit in private imo, just not in public.

62182448
>>62182409
Then how come 90% of people are poor?

62182449
>>62181824
Great, as soon as you slip up you're fired, thanks bye

62182461
>>62182449
I simply won't slip up. Brb it's time for my hourly bowel movement. Sucks for you LOL

And to think all you had to do was respect your wagies

62182475
>>62182427
What was the issue with him sitting? His pace was just the same and due to hi unusual height he was able to reach everything just fine. These were 16-24 hour shifts mind you, 100hr work weeks

62182497
Wait until the middle management shills ITT find out about Porn at Work Wednesdays

62182525
>>62182461
What gets me is when team leads make a big show when they don't have the power to write up anyone or even the fact that none of the shit is falling down on them. Some people just get off on being in "charge" of others.

62182537
>>62181666
>uses 3 rolls of toilet paper to make the seat level again
Not My Fucking Problem.

62182610
Man I always feel so comfy reading these threads from my work from home lake house, caught a big bass last Thursday while on long boring teams meeting from my dock

62182685
>>62182610
lol legit cope post

62183229
>>62181737
Lmao cope harder cuck

62184087
I think we should start banning employees from having caffeine too. It, like sitting, is too convenient and makes the work day too easy, am I right?

62184093
>>62181666
>Turns my 30 minutes toilet breaks into free leg workout
Sign me the fuck up

62184233
Why the fuck do blue collar workers get treated like shit compared to white collar ones?

I've worked all types of jobs (retail, warehouse, white collar office, etc.) and the double standards are insane. In my last warehouse job you literally could not be a single second over your break time. You could not even idle or take a breather if you finished your allotted work (because you were just faster/better at your job) so they find some other random shit for you to do. They penalize you for working harder.

Meanwhile in my current fake email job I can just leave my desk and go for a walk anytime I want. I can disappear for 30 mins to go to the grocery store. No one cares.

62185961
Fuck target wagies

62185969
>>62181734
that sounds like hell

item.title: 80K WAITING ROOM — 23 replies — 39.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
80K WAITING ROOM

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-05-04 04:15 UTC

OP

80K WAITING ROOM

Early replies (first 3)

62185820
>>62185816
Yup yup yuip

62185821
>>62185816
Delusional. See you at 40k.

62185826
linkbtc
complete market dominance

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62185828
>>62185816
YES PLEASE

62185830
waiting to dick down the brown

62185832
cool but i bought at 125k

62185834
>>62185832
That was almost a year ago
Why didn't you buy at 60k?

62185843
>>62185832
Me too lmao

62185862
>>62185816
$80,000!!!! WOOOOO!!
WE DID IT!!!

62185867
price EOY?

62185868
>>62185816

62185869
>>62185867
How does 670k sound

62185879
>>62185869
>670k
I'm okay with a clean $1.5m after taxes tbdesu

62185880
>>62185867
200k

62185883
>>62185816
more like 81k waiting room am I right boys? hahahahahahaha

62185887
>>62185867
Just by looking at its chart I'd say $32k

62185891
>>62185816
Anyone know what is controlling the price?
Multiple exchanges
All with different wallets
No centralization
No supply and demand factor
Just numbers on screens
Bank accounts being emptied into other bank accounts for “crypto coins”
Can buy it at a gas station ATM
Can buy it through most brokerage firms
Just a clown world I live in a fake reality

62185893
fomo'd. How high we going?

62185910
>>62185893

62185921
>>62185893
He fomo'd?

62186026
WHAT THE FUCK WAS THAT

62186043
>>62185816
wahey!

item.title: admit it linkies. admit that you're not special. all you did was buy some stupid token and got lucky. but then, in a cru… — 25 replies — 29.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
admit it linkies. admit that you're not special. all you did was buy some stupid token and got lucky. but then, in a cru…

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-05-04 03:59 UTC

OP

admit it linkies. admit that you're not special. all you did was buy some stupid token and got lucky. but then, in a cruel twist of fate, it turns out the coin you devoted your life to was actually 90% hype. and now you are poor. reduced to normal faggotry and mediocrity, where you belong. admit these things and then there will be a path to redemption. but if you keep living in denial, you will wallow in your pathetic misery forever. the choice is yours. i am trying to help you. ALL of us are trying to help you.

Early replies (first 3)

62180296
>>62180275
No

62180322
>>62180275
"all of us" are using Chainlink - when Sergey compares the token to buying in to Linux, he was right.

62180323
what was with the fuddies getting so mind broken they started spamming gay porno couple nights back lmao, can't be real

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62180368
>>62180322
Nobody made money off of Linux.

62180382
>>62180275
My cousin unironically works for chainl*nk and it's fucking OVER

62180384
they're trying to force the linkbtc downtrend but they will have to crash bitcoin if they want any blood from this stone, no one is selling lmao

62180392
>>62180275
LINK is just like all the other shitty altcoin scams that fade into obscurity. The morons thought they were special

62180413
>>62180368
because linux didn't create a token

62180639
Why is this board so obsessed with chainlink?

62180669
HOLY SHIT

By sheer chance I happened to be watching Sunny today, "The gang finds a dumpster baby", and recognised one of the frames Asslarper used (pic related).

In this episode, Dennis (AB's avatar) convinces an activist to CHAINLINK himself to a tree, then LITERALLY cuckolds him.

>A SINGULAR TREE
>THE cuckOLDS OF CRYPTO!!!

Now do you believe me??

Inverse Assblaster; what did he tell you was useless?

62180680
>entire fud campaign based on some vc seething about ((("being wrong")))
kek

62180684
>>62180275
Hello mentally ill faggot how are you doing today?

62180685
>>62180639
99% of this boards wealth is in LINK

62180698
Its gonna be fine
People were dunking on the chainlink build rewards, rightly so because of value, but they also laughed because we had to do a quiz.

Turns out that chainlink knew about legislative action months ago before it actually got settled. We're in the whitehouse, we're gonna get our commodity status, basically every prediction market that matters adopted link. If one could argue there is one infra play in crypto, its chainlink. The only thing you're betting on is how big the institutional crypto market is gonna get, which at that point is a sure bet of substantial increase no matter what copers will say

62180700
>>62180680
>>62180684
>>62180323
hey cuckolds, check the price :)

62180702
>>62180698
Amundis spiko fund is secured by chainlink and has arguably been one of the winners in that category, its gonna be fine

Lots of replies will be just straight up coping by fudders who hold link themselves

62180728
>>62180413
If they did, it would be as useless as BAT

62180736
>>62180680
>they were wrong
>$9
insane cuckold cope

62180801
>>62180275
israel won

62181236
>>62180698
>they also laughed because we had to do a quiz.
rightly so, stinkbag

62181283
>>62180680
>>entire fud campaign based on some vc seething about ((("being wrong")))
qrd? what's the vc in question?

62181930
>>62181283
Adem is the CEO VC of the based department

62182244
>>62180275
the real joke is on you fuddie, you never wanted to be right, you wanted to be wrong,

you wanted link to succeed, but you fudded it into single digits.

the real joke is on you, you wanted to be wrong, but you were right.

now yu suffer more than the marines

62183887
fuck all stinks

62186006
zappity beebop

item.title: Gentrification of the entire world — 17 replies — 27.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Gentrification of the entire world

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-05-04 04:13 UTC

OP

I remember seeing these 10 years ago when I was into boating. It's a hollow surfboard with a weedeater engine and a little jet drive so you can scoot around on the water. The inventor was showing off the 1st gen units, they cost something like $3k USD which I thought was expensive at the time, how could ordinary people afford that? My brain at the time couldn't comprehend just how fucked the future could possibly get.

These things now cost $10k+ euros,15k+ USD, $20k+ CAD. There's electric versions too, they should be cheap right? Just a battery and a motor, no, it's priced at $21k CAD.

Hoverboards and electric scooters weren't much of a thing at the time either, but china made them by the millions and made them cheap enough so that anyone could afford mobility. These guys went down the opposite route.

Something has gone very wrong with our civilization, instead of trying to make things as afforadble as possible so that everyone can have access to new experiences and a better quality of life which leads to a stronger community through shared culture, our companies have gone down the route of increasing prices to be as high as possible so a small handful of very rich people can enjoy life while the masses fade into irrelevance. In the past this business model would not have been sustainable because of a lack of capital, but now there's so much printed money floating around chasing returns that it seems that any kind of predatory business that overcharges for 'luxury' is viable.

How much longer can this continue?

Early replies (first 3)

62184907
>>62184899
who cares?

buy a chinese inflatable boat if your poor and leave the carbon fiber titanium 2 stroke jetsurfs for the elite

62184959
>>62184899
The top 10% do 50% of the consumption. Might as well cater everything to them.

62184976
>>62184907
This is what I mean, they make you think it's a premium luxury item by using marketing like carbon fiber and titanium. Neither of these materials are actually expensive or necessary. The cost in carbon fiber is mainly from the hand layup process, which on a part the size of a small surf board isn't much and you could just as easily use fiberglass like all marine stuff. And titanium while pricier than aluminum doesn't justify a $20,000 price tag and has no benefits in this particular application.

These boards should realistically cost about $1400 if they were mass manufactured but instead it's easier for them to keep it as a boutique product and sell way less of them for alot more money.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62184978
>>62184899
they are free if you just steal one

62184982
>>62184899
>$3k USD which I thought was expensive
Just how poor are you? What special knowledge did you have at the time that convinced you that your opinion was more valid and informed than the actual manufacturers? And why do you think the world owes you access to every little frivolity that happens to catch your eyes at whatever price you think they should charge? Just how narcissistic are you, and did you take your meds today?
>These guys went down the opposite route.
Perhaps if you think *really* hard about the inherent differences between a scooter and a surfboard, the inherent differences between their use cases, as well as the sizes and demographics of their respective potential markets, you might realize the *really* *fucking* *obvious* differences between them that led their manufacturers on their opposite routes. Or just sit there and seethe ignorantly, I don't really care.

62185191
>>62184982
I bet I'm way richer than you if we're talking FIAT. The special knowledge of knowing how things are made and what prices mass market goods would bear, the business model of chasing only the ultra wealthy didn't exist for stuff like this at the time. You can make one of these by hand from scratch for $3-4k, even cheaper if you harvest an electric scooter, charging $20k shouldn't be possible in a real capitalist economy.

The inherent differences in the markets exist because of the pricing model, no one had electric scooters 15 years ago. I remember because I got one given to me for free, it was many thousands of dollars when new and ran off of lead acid batteries. Once the batteries died they threw it away because the technology was shit an a PITA to deal with. Hoverboards and escooters took off after it became cheap enough for the average person to afford them, before that it was mainly out of touch wealthy faggots on $$$ segways.

62185206
why would i care if some richfags or poorfags with credits wants to surf on the sea

you guys really need to grow up ffs also this board is not your personal blog fuck your mother

62185216
>>62185191
>You can make one of these by hand from scratch for $3-4k
Then make your own then, and stop your whining about how unfair life is that nobody is giving you one for free. You aren't special, everybody is their mommy's bestest little boy.

62185228
>>62185191
>charging $20k shouldn't be possible in a real capitalist economy
Serious question - just how stupid are you? Or are you a turdie, or a 14yo incel that's never left your mother's boyfriend's basement? Because those are the only 3 types of people that could so confidently make such an utterly and obviously wrong claim and not instantly die of shame.

62185250
>>62185216
Retard, I don't want one of these fucking things. I'm saying that there isn't half a car worth of parts and labor in these toys.

>>62185228
I don't think you even have a job. You can buy a 2-3 year old car for $20k or you can get a fucking surfboard toy? That does not make sense in any reality.

62185272
Imagine this OP

Senior Finance Analysts on Jane Street received 2.8 million dollars each last year in total comp.

Meanwhile you have people fighting for meme bank teller jobs that require college degrees and pay 23/hr "with benefits".

Do you really not understand? The world has been divided into the 10% of americans with a net worth of a million, and those who don't.

62185304
>>62185272
99.99999% of americans arent sernior finance analysts at jane street

62185330
>>62184899
>These things now cost $10k+ euros,15k+ USD, $20k+ CAD. There's electric versions too, they should be cheap right? Just a battery and a motor, no, it's priced at $21k CAD.
make cheaper sell become millionaire lose virginity. oh wait have to get out of bed first instead collect gibs complain 4pebble

62185385
>>62185304
10% are millionaires (net worth)
Many of those are boomers with reverse mortgages, spending it all before they die

The market is tending to the needs of the 10%, not the 90%.

62185637
>>62184982
Take it easy, rabbi

62185864
Overcharging for luxury items is an ancient marketing trick. Boaters bitch about how everything related to their hobby is priced in "boat bucks," but it's only like that because if you charged them a fair price they would assume it's a crappy product and refuse to buy it.

62186038
>>62184978
A True Businessman

item.title: No one is talking about how there's no jobs. — 42 replies — 21.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
No one is talking about how there's no jobs.

Open thread • Replies: 42 • Updated: 2026-05-04 02:53 UTC

OP

No one is talking about how there's no jobs. Ive been working for 10 years and never seen the market nearly this fucked up. Every job is fake, they will interview for 3 or 4 rounds and still repost the job next week, after ghosting people of course. People irl are coping so hard. Im back for my masters and not one person has any job or internship in any of my classes this or previous semester except me(and I will get laid off soon). These zoomers are so fucking deluded. They cant get an internship so they say "of well, im just gonna focus on finishing my masters". Is this how THEY are getting away with it? Society has already brainwashed people so badly the people in charge can drink their milkshake and leave them destitute and they wont even notice?

Early replies (first 3)

62184289
>>62184286
skill issue. ggf &c

62184301
>>62184289
All these interviews just happened to not like me or anyone else they interviewed and reposted the job? What a coincidence. Crazy how i had skills for the past 10 years and always instantly found a job but something randomly happened and now I lost whatever skills I have as I got good excperince in my fields

62184315
>>62184301
Oh and here is the email from my on-site interview on may 4th i got on friday. Nevermind it was my drram company and this tore my fucking hear out. Skill issue on my part that they canceled the role..

Oh and this is only 2/3 of all my interviews this past months if u count phone calls

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62184413
>>62184286
you suck and no one wants to pay for your work

US unemployment is 4%.... you are in the bottom 4%.

62184461
>>62184413
>doesn't understand what the unemployment figure measures.

based retard

62184496
>>62184413
fuck off retard EVERY JOB SECTOR is struggling with ghost jobs and unemployment

kys

62184529
>>62184413
Im currently employed but I haven't been looking for a new job for months cuz I sense a layoff

62184533
>>62184461
How do you interpret it? There is no way its 4 percent when not on person in any of my classes has an internship

62184556
>>62184286
I just got a job here last week
>90k+ salary
>IT Management role, ideally IT Director
>full time, salary, complete package of benefits

What you did was not question things and follow what the people who wanted you to be a grunt told you to do. What I did was self improve and do jobs that exposed me to alot of industries and people started seeking me out to get their problems solved. Do you see where you fucked up?
>Protip: College is a scam, companies will hire you over college normies if you know your shit and do good work, both of which a college student cant provide proof of outside "le meme certificate"
Reality is an IQ test, what do you think your IQ is?

62184660
>>62184556
OP here, if i quit my job 90k would be a horrible blow to my current finances as I consider myself financially destitute at 110k. Which is why im looking for a job. What happens when a guy that knows his shir and does good work AND has a college degree in your field?

62184677
>>62184315
Translation: Thank you for your data, which we will now sell to third parties. So long, suckers

62184690
>>62184660
someone who actually went to college should have learned to network with others, as that is the 2nd part you go to college for. Did you not network with anyone? Keep in mind I self studied and did projects that I was interested in, which employers value 100x more than college completion. Did you do any projects in College that you would put into a portfolio? You sound like you are in a high cost of living area. 90k to a low cost of living area gives me plenty of comfort room to do whatever and take care of all needs with plenty left over.
>knows his shit, does good work and college degree
If you have the first two, there should have bene people who are coming to you to have you do work. If this isnt the case, then you're overexaggerating yourself. Those who do good work and know their shit will never actually run into no jobs, unless their work is in an empty industry that has no demand. I didn't even interview, they knew they wanted me and hired me as soon as they could.
>What experiences have you had that defines you as knowing your shit AND does good work?

62185003
>>62184301
>All these interviews just happened to not like me
Yes. It's you, not them. You are the common factor in all of those rejections.
>i had skills for the past 10 years
You skill - such as it was - was being a warm body to fill a seat for the bean counters who conflated head count for productivity (because that's the sort of category error that accountants always make). That skill is no longer particularly valuable.

62185045
>>62184690
My freind did oxy for 10 years, got sober, lied on his resume and passed a compTA. He's a networking engineer now. U arent special. Ill just take ur job when I fully give up

62185047
>>62184496
Yeah 4% unemployment, roughly the same level of unemployment as it's been the last 2 decades. There are plenty of jobs out there, and plenty of people getting hired into those jobs. I changed jobs just last year, made one call, had one interview, and got an offer before I walked out the door. 18% raise from my previous position. But I'm actually good at what I do, it's not just some delusion that I use to prop up my persecution complex.

So yes, it really is a skill issue, and you should git gud, you fucking faggot.

62185053
>>62185003
You mis read what I said on purpose. Since they reposted the job in a bootlicker retard world like urs that would mean that no one they interviewed was good enough. Which is insane of course. Why do u bootlicker so hard? I was a bootlicker just like you and it got me nowhere, you will find our soon enough zoomie

62185055
>>62185047
How do yoy explines emails like this?>>62184315
Im employed now but im not gonna larp as if everything is okay. Have fun getting laid off alongside me in a few months

62185058
>>62185047
Also. I got a job too last year, that was last year retard. Shit has changed, u retard.


Also if u were better than everyone else, then you would realize the exception doesnt make the rule.

62185125
>>62185053
>no one they interviewed was good enough
Yes. It happens a lot and this isn't a recent phenomenon as you seem to believe. I've seen it happen from the other side, you get a tranche of candidates in to interview and none of them are suitable, maybe one guy has the chops but has an attitude problem, another guy turns out to be a good bullshitter, another guy got good grades in his classes but doesn't seem to remember a thing about what was covered in them, &c. So you put out the position again.
>>62185058
>the exception doesnt make the rule.
You should think very hard about that before making a fool of yourself on the internet. 4% unemployment. *You* are the exception. *You* and a very few other people are undesireable. It's not a conspiracy against you (no matter how better that makes you feel), it is your fault that you are having trouble getting a job.
>>62185055
Positions do close without being filled. It could be worse, he could have been hired for the job, quit his current job, then get laid off before ever getting his first paycheck. It happened to a junior coworker of mine back in 1992, he was doing the junior job-hopping thing climbing the salary ladder and hopped right off a cliff. He was able to get back his previous job at our company because we had started interviewing but hadn't filled it yet, but took a pay cut because he was desperate and we knew he was gonna job hop again at the first opportunity. And yes all those prospective interviewees got their rejection letter and a notice that the position was closed. Shit happens.

62185134
>>62185058
>Shit has changed
It really hasn't, you're just too young and naive to realize it. Nihil sub sole novum.

62185164
>>62184286
just wait. they is doing a stimulus soon.

62185658
>>62185045
Quite the projection. I have to deny people because I get so much work... yet here you are having issues finding work. The answer is in what you are experiencing; since there are many in YOUR position and you are competing with them and not getting it, that means you are AT MOST average. What projects have you worked on? I mentioned a portfolio earlier but you completely avoided that. I hope the context im providing you is at least getting through your ego to help you identify the actual issue here: you

62185675
>>62184286
Hella people talk about this.
Not the media, because they fucking help the govt lie about the economy

62185684
>>62185125
> Unemployment is at 4%
So that means there aren't many jobs available.
Why are you treating him like he's wrong then? It's hard to find a real, well-paying job

62185696
>>62184406
My coked out Mexican manager showed me it's faster to dump large amounts of the hot water, *scrub scrub scrub*, push water into floor drain, then dip, mop, dip, squeeze excess, dry up any excess water on the floor with dry mop, and then you're done
Very efficient in a restaurant

62185702
>>62185684
>So that means there aren't many jobs available.
Nope, that means there are plenty of jobs available. 4% is at the low end of the unemployment rate that is considered "full employment" (the baseline level of unemployment to be expected even when there are significantly more jobs than job seekers).

62185755
I get blown up by recruiters non stop

t. Lawyer

62185815
>>62185125
>working since 1992

no wonder your a fucking boomer hahahaha

62185827
>>62184286
There's this one big local company I interviewed for in November. I was highly qualified, 10 years experience in the field...and they never got back to me. A month later I saw the listing vanish...figured they found someone, whatever. Until a couple weeks later it popped back up again, same position, same pay, same location.

I called the hiring manager and asked him about it, and how I never did get a confirmation one way or the other about the job and he stammered a bit. Said he'd get back to me when he figured it out.

After I hung up I got an email saying I wasn't being considered anymore and the job once again vanished.

Two months later, the job listing popped back up again. Same position, same pay, same location. It's still up there too. For 8 months already.

62185829
Always reminds me how every boomer's life story is like
>I wanted something bigger than working in a factory.... so I became a lawyer/businessowner/military general/rock star
Or some shit. Like it was a choice, you just lazily chose to work in a factory at a union job.... or became a fucking rockstar.

62185833
>>62185815
every poster who says they're a 60yo lawyer or whatever since 1992 is just jebaiting you anyway.

62185878
Tried running my own landscaping bizness this year. Zero customers. Oversaturated market, can't compete with Juan working for $2 a hour. Gave up on applying for jobs. Feels like a loosh harvest. I'll Uber and do handy work to get by.

>>62184410
Property tax will push Americans over the edge, just watch. Evictions will skyrocket.

>>62184396
I broke my back. Got arthritis in my right hip in my 20s working landscaping + general contracting. Never had benefits. Have no health insurance now. Its so fucking over

>>62185164
DAS RIIIIGHHTTTT

62185888
>>62185829
>Like it was a choice
As the poet once said "If you decide not to decide you still have made a choice."

item.title: >be Joby >spent 8 years getting FAA cert >stage 4 cleared, FAA test pilots about to fly the thing >first eVTOL in histor… — 14 replies — 16.7/hr

OP

OH OH I SMELL A STINKY

Lol. Go buy an MD500 like a real chad

62185825

62185836

62185840

62185966
>>62185018

62185967

>t. Has played Sim Copter religiously for almost 30 years

62185989

NOTARs just look so fucking ugly. I want an MD 500 with a round nose not that shitty pointy nose

62186008

item.title: Woman JP Morgan executive Rapes and turns Male Employee into sex slave — 160 replies — 29.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Woman JP Morgan executive Rapes and turns Male Employee into sex slave

Open thread • Replies: 160 • Updated: 2026-05-03 23:26 UTC

OP

https://x.com/dailymail/status/2049618941043949691?s=46

A white woman JP Morgan Executive (mega international bank) drugged and raped a married man before taking pictures of him to use as blackmail.

62184054
I have not had a extensive look into the story yet.
But here is a funny aspect.

Her name Hajdini
originates from:
The surname Hajdini is of Albanian origin, rooted in the Balkans,
It is derived from the word hajdin,
which historically referred to an outlaw or brigand

They were outlaws used in europe to fight Arabs who wanted to conquer Europe
(A different way to write it is Hajduk
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hajduk
The etymology of the word hajduk is unclear. One theory is that hajduk was derived from the Turkish word haidut or haydut 'bandit',
... because the Turkish word haidut or haydut is adapted from the Hungarian hajtó or hajdó
Other spellings in English include ajduk, haydut, haiduk, haiduc, hayduck, and hayduk.

It is interesting because aren't there posts where she allegedly refers
to his arab origin ...
